content: Build a Slack integration that can send messages to channels and list channels using a bot token

Great choice! Here's what I'm setting up for your Slack integration:

- **Send Message** — Post a text message to any channel your bot has access to
- **List Channels** — Retrieve a list of all public channels in your workspace

You can always come back to add more actions later.

---

## How to get your credentials

1. Go to [https://api.slack.com/apps](https://api.slack.com/apps) and sign in with your Slack account
2. Click **Create New App**, choose **From scratch**, give it a name (e.g. "Ekoa Bot"), and select your workspace
3. In the left sidebar, click **OAuth & Permissions**
4. Scroll down to **Bot Token Scopes** and add these scopes:
   - `chat:write` — allows sending messages
   - `channels:read` — allows listing public channels
5. Scroll back up and click **Install to Workspace**, then confirm
6. Copy the **Bot User OAuth Token** — it starts with `xoxb-`
7. Finally, invite your bot to any channel you want it to post in by typing `/invite @YourBotName` in that channel

---

## Testing your integration

Your integration is now ready in the panel on the right.

Go to the **Tests** tab, enter your Bot Token, select an action, and click **Run Test**.

- Try **List Channels** first — you should see a list of your workspace's public channels returned. That confirms the token works.
- Then try **Send Message** — use `#general` as the channel and write something like `Hello from Ekoa!` as the text. Check Slack to confirm the message appears.

A successful response will show `"ok": true` along with the message or channel data.

---

```skill-md
---
name: slack
description: Use this integration to send messages to Slack channels or retrieve a list of channels in the workspace. Ideal for notifications, alerts, and workflow updates.
---

# Slack

Connects to Slack via a Bot Token to send messages and list channels within a workspace.

## Security Model
All actions are executed through the Ekoa Integration Proxy. The Bot Token is stored encrypted on the company server and is never exposed to the AI or logged anywhere.

## Available Actions

### send_message
Sends a text message to a Slack channel. The bot must be invited to the channel before it can post.

**Arguments:**
- `channel` (string, required): The channel name (e.g. `#general`) or channel ID (e.g. `C012AB3CD`)
- `text` (string, required): The message text to send. Supports Slack markdown (bold with `*text*`, code with `` `code` ``)
- `username` (string, optional): Override the bot display name for this message. Defaults to the bot's configured name.

**Examples:**

Basic usage:
```
integration_execute("slack", "send_message", { channel: "#general", text: "Deployment to production completed successfully." })
```

With a custom username:
```
integration_execute("slack", "send_message", { channel: "#alerts", text: "*Warning:* Disk usage above 90% on prod-server-01", username: "Ekoa Alerts" })
```

**Expected response:**
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "channel": "C012AB3CD",
  "ts": "1512085950.000216",
  "message": {
    "text": "Deployment to production completed successfully.",
    "username": "Ekoa Bot",
    "type": "message"
  }
}
```

**Common errors:**
- `channel_not_found`: The channel name or ID is incorrect, or the bot has not been invited to the channel
- `not_in_channel`: Invite the bot to the channel first with `/invite @YourBotName`
- `invalid_auth`: The bot token is incorrect or has been revoked

---

### list_channels
Returns a list of public channels in the Slack workspace. Use this to discover channel names and IDs.

**Arguments:**
- `limit` (number, optional): Maximum number of channels to return. Defaults to 100. Max is 1000.
- `exclude_archived` (boolean, optional): Set to `true` to hide archived channels. Defaults to `true`.

**Examples:**

Basic usage:
```
integration_execute("slack", "list_channels", {})
```

With filters:
```
integration_execute("slack", "list_channels", { limit: 200, exclude_archived: true })
```

**Expected response:**
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "channels": [
    { "id": "C012AB3CD", "name": "general", "is_channel": true, "num_members": 42 },
    { "id": "C034EF5GH", "name": "engineering", "is_channel": true, "num_members": 15 }
  ],
  "response_metadata": { "next_cursor": "" }
}
```

**Common errors:**
- `missing_scope`: The bot token is missing the `channels:read` scope — re-install the app with the correct scopes
- `invalid_auth`: The bot token is incorrect or has been revoked

## Configuration Requirements
- **Bot Token** (Required) [Secret]: The Bot User OAuth Token from your Slack app. Starts with `xoxb-`. Found at api.slack.com/apps → your app → OAuth & Permissions.

## Error Handling
- `ACCESS_DENIED`: The bot lacks the required OAuth scope for this action
- `NOT_CONFIGURED`: Admin has not entered the Bot Token yet
- `channel_not_found`: Channel does not exist or bot is not a member
```

```config-json
{
  "version": "2.0",
  "skillType": "integration",
  "integrationKey": "slack",
  "displayName": "Slack",
  "description": "Send messages to Slack channels and list workspace channels using a Bot Token.",
  "authType": "api_key",
  "provider": "Slack",
  "category": "communication",
  "configSchema": [
    {
      "key": "bot_token",
      "label": "Bot Token",
      "type": "password",
      "required": true,
      "secret": true,
      "helpText": "Your Slack Bot User OAuth Token. Starts with 'xoxb-'. Found at api.slack.com/apps → your app → OAuth & Permissions → Bot User OAuth Token."
    }
  ],
  "actions": [
    {
      "actionName": "send_message",
      "description": "Send a text message to a Slack channel",
      "mutates": true,
      "argsSchema": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "channel": { "type": "string", "description": "Channel name (e.g. #general) or channel ID" },
          "text": { "type": "string", "description": "Message text to send" },
          "username": { "type": "string", "description": "Override bot display name for this message" }
        },
        "required": ["channel", "text"]
      },
      "returnSchema": { "type": "object" },
      "httpConfig": {
        "method": "POST",
        "baseUrl": "https://slack.com",
        "path": "/api/chat.postMessage",
        "headers": {
          "Authorization": "Bearer {{bot_token}}",
          "Content-Type": "application/json; charset=utf-8"
        },
        "bodyTemplate": {
          "channel": "{{channel}}",
          "text": "{{text}}",
          "username": "{{username}}"
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "actionName": "list_channels",
      "description": "List public channels in the Slack workspace",
      "mutates": false,
      "argsSchema": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "limit": { "type": "number", "description": "Maximum number of channels to return (default 100, max 1000)" },
          "exclude_archived": { "type": "boolean", "description": "Exclude archived channels (default true)" }
        },
        "required": []
      },
      "returnSchema": { "type": "object" },
      "httpConfig": {
        "method": "GET",
        "baseUrl": "https://slack.com",
        "path": "/api/conversations.list",
        "headers": {
          "Authorization": "Bearer {{bot_token}}"
        },
        "queryParams": {
          "limit": "{{limit}}",
          "exclude_archived": "{{exclude_archived}}"
        }
      }
    }
  ],
  "proxyContract": {
    "executeEndpoint": "/api/agent/integrations/execute",
    "requiredInputs": ["integrationKey", "actionName", "args"]
  }
}
```
